programmes.show_description: A teenager is the prime suspect when a 12-year-old boy is fatally stabbed in a park. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

11/15. Emily hopes to do pottery full-time, but a lack of clarity in her designs is holding her back. Momoka's weaving is stunning, but she needs her craft to pay to support her family.

The red and blue teams find out whether they bought wisely when their items sell at auction in Lyme Bay. Both experts take a punt on small bonus buys, but for one, the reward is big!

programmes.show_name: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programmes.show_description: 4/6. Stacey and the team travel to Milton Keynes to help Bianca and Ollie Parej, parents of five children under nine, transform their cramped and chaotic home.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
Oscar-winning epic. A doctor and poet falls in love with the mistress of a political opportunist, but their romance is disrupted by the social upheaval surrounding them. [1965]
Actress and cake expert Jane Asher brings together a selection of recipes from the nation's favourite television chefs.
20/26. Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts presented by the public in Melksham, Wiltshire. Among the items are a Cossack dagger and a drawing by Edmund Dulac.

The Eye team investigates Chinese disciplinary schools promising to 'fix' problem children at a devastating cost and Mariko Oi is in Singapore.
2/6. The UK's oldest female Ironman triathlete is immortalised in paint.

programmes.show_name: Lost and Found in the Lakes
